item.content:encoded:
I write for the GRU-P site.
It fits very well with its theme.

item.title: Staff Post -- Open
item.content:encoded:
timer removed

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show whatever
- Hide whatever

Editing footnote from this:

GRU Division "P"

To include a link to the GOI page.

GRU Division "P"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It isn't, no. According to my research back in 2013, Os Sumum was supposed to mean "We Are Mouth" in Latin. I seem to recall it being suggested by someone rather than Wiktionary, Google Translate or another online source.

If you have a better suggestion, I'm happy to hear it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is os sumum just a name you came up with? I noticed os means 'mouth' in latin so Ive been doing some searching but can't seem to find the word 'sumum' in any languages..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While this post is, to put it mildly, rather old, I can't help to address it now that I have noticed it - to my defense, I was not around in any meaningful sense when it was made, as I would have done so then.

ACME foldable treason-hammer
Just 10 000 000 Marks

To put it bluntly, the above view is at best a misguided attempt that went way too far in terms of trying to assuage his (and Daladier's) guilt, and at worst, mentioning a portion of the facts to excuse something rather vile (even though understandable).

Now, let it be said that ultimately, the fault for what has happened lies to a large extent on the shoulders of Dr. Edvard Benes - he forced his relatively well defended, and battle-ready country (Czechoslovakia has gotten its statehood two decades ago, and large sections of the population were willing to fight to defend it, as they recalled what living under foreign oppression was like, not the least), to stand down, and of course himself fled to Great Britain from where he pretended to be in charge, and ultimately made everyone's lives more difficult - for example, the bombing of the Apollo refinery in June 1944 was carried out despite the local resistance taking pains to inform him as it would have played a significant role in material reinforcement of the Slovak National Uprising, which took place a bit over a month later.)

However, the conduct of the two named gentlemen, while perhaps understandable ,is by no means praiseworthy.1

First of all - despite Czechoslovakia being a nominal ally of Great Britain and France, and a number of our units having fought on their side during WWI, they have excluded Czechoslovak representatives from any meaningful negotiations, presenting them with pretty much a "fait accompli", in the famous "O nás bez nás." (about us without us) manner that would repeat itself a few more times through our history.

Second, and closely related is a second issue - rather than plainly stating that if a conflict occurs between Germany and Czechoslovakia, they will stay neutral, which would have been a self-interested but essentially honest stance, they presented the whole issue in a manner that pressured the Czechoslovak government towards agreeing with Hitler's land claims. I really haven't yet seen a single cogent defense or explanation of this approach, maybe except for the explanation that the local bourgeoisie was afraid of USSR and was hoping Hitler would make a barrier to that. Well, either that, or they were just commanding assholes, tbh.

Thirdly, there's the whole issue of war readiness. Yes, UK was reluctant to go to war, and yes, they had lost a significant amount of their youth during WWI (and the same goes for France) - however, they weren't the only one, or even the one worst hit.

The Soviet Union offered Czechoslovakia help, despite having endured much higher losses
in number during WWI, and having been subject to a further war - the 1918-1924 war against the White Guards. A war, that, it bears to note, did have significant foreign involvement from UK, USA and other imperialist countries, but I digress.

Furthermore, Yugoslavia, whose main constituent Serbia has lost between 11-18% of its total population, the losses running as high numerically as that of the several times larger UK, throughout WWI has offered military help in case that Czechoslovakia would defend itself. While it'd be foolish to expect such action from other states, it does put the actions (and excuses of actions) of misters Chamberlain and Daladier in an entirely different light.

I could go on about how the strategic situation of Czechoslovakia, both in terms of border defense and industrial capacity was far from hopeless (Yes, there would have been likely territorial losses in the case of a war, especially things like an attack through Austria, but our chances were better than good), but this goes more to shame Dr. Benes than the previously mentioned.


Ultimately , it was this conduct mentioned, this going well above and beyond what was reasonable in his situation, that makes this article work out as an amusing fictional-historical perspective, and yet another reason why I have enthusiastically upvoted it.
Footnotes
1. Just as a reminder - what they did bit them in the arses majorly from a strategic perspective, however, I don't need to stoop so low as to use the famous 20/20 eye of hindsight to criticize their actions. They have given me plenty rope to hang them with anyhow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit of a necropost, but I second this.

For the record, you'll note this was actually addressed already and got Mulciber's upvote.

If I may be so bold…remember that these articles are, in-universe, supposed to be documentations of highly anomalous things, not entertainment. The Foundation would almost certainly want to put a description of the procedure in the document, so as to document all the anomalous things about the anomalous thing they're documenting, you see?

Aside from being incredibly patronizing, that's an opinion, not fact. A) No, the Foundation does not want secondary documents in the main body of their documentation. This is literally why supplements exist. B) There is such a document detailing the ritual, and it's mentioned in the article, so I'm not sure what your complaint is. That it's not spelled out in this document? No, it's not and it's not going to be.

In-universe, it makes no sense to leave the procedure unexplained

It wasn't, read the article again.

I can see the intended-creepypasta-based rationale behind your reasoning, but part of what makes SCP articles so creepy is that they actually make sense in-universe as scientific documents (apart from being impossible). The idea that there's a world where the SCP Foundation exists - needs to exist - is disturbing on its own, and making the articles make as much sense as possible for that universe is the underlying creepiness that ties all SCP articles together. Am I making sense?

You are, but you're saying exactly the opposite of what you mean. In-universe, the ritual is documented and available. Researchers with clearance can go look at supplemental procedure 1118/D/Ax-14:Z3. Out here in reality, the procedure has not been spelled out, because that's not essential to the document. In fact, it would ruin it. So, you're complaining about it not being explained in-universe, when in fact you're miffed about you, the user, not being able to read it.

Furthermore, if the procedure is so horrible for the targets, how would the targets be made targets of without them knowing about it at all? They should notice something so incredibly "not nice". My imagination can't run with that, it simply makes no sense.

That's quite possibly the biggest non sequitur I've read in a while. No, that's not a logical conclusion. You're trying to apply logic to a document about a typewriter with teeth for keys, that needs a dark pagan ritual to bind it to a target. What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bit of a necropost, but I second this. I was unable to enjoy most of the article or even pay any attention to any of the stuff in the second addendum because by then I was just skimming looking for a description of the "procedure". If I may be so bold…remember that these articles are, in-universe, supposed to be documentations of highly anomalous things, not entertainment. The Foundation would almost certainly want to put a description of the procedure in the document, so as to document all the anomalous things about the anomalous thing they're documenting, you see? In-universe, it makes no sense to leave the procedure unexplained - I can see the intended-creepypasta-based rationale behind your reasoning, but part of what makes SCP articles so creepy is that they actually make sense in-universe as scientific documents (apart from being impossible). The idea that there's a world where the SCP Foundation exists - needs to exist - is disturbing on its own, and making the articles make as much sense as possible for that universe is the underlying creepiness that ties all SCP articles together. Am I making sense?

Furthermore, if the procedure is so horrible for the targets, how would the targets be made targets of without them knowing about it at all? They should notice something so incredibly "not nice". My imagination can't run with that, it simply makes no sense.

Personally, no vote, due to the above-described irritants. And for what it's worth, that's rare for me. Take it how you will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still at a loss as to why you seem to believe it is a) an addition for color (it's an integral part of the narrative) and b) possible to be historically accurate when what's under scrutiny is a direct effect of the scip the article is about.

Anyway, let's drop this, all we can agree on is that we disagree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that the concept and even the article itself could exist without incorporating historical inaccuracies like that. It's an addition for color that breaks the immersion by being wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, the point is the historical inaccuracy. No, Chamberlain did not appease the Germans because he was a secret fascist, he didn't do it because he liked their sausages, and he certainly didn't do it because of an occult artifact typewriter with molars for keys. I'm not sure what your point is or where you think I'm Chamberlain-bashing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoting due to the inaccurate Chamberlain-bashing. Do you know why Chamberlain appeased Hitler? Because Britain was nowhere near ready to fight the Nazis. He wasn't submitting to the Nazis; at best, he was buying time for England to prepare for the coming onslaught, and for more allies to be swayed to the anti-Nazi cause.

http://www.telegraph.co.uk/history/world-war-two/6062452/Neville-Chamberlain-should-be-praised-not-buried.html

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That depends on how you define interaction. Certainly there's users from the translation sites with an account here, and there's certainly communication between the wikis thanks to SCP International, but I don't think there's a lot of communication, let alone synchronization on the content level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There isn't much interaction between the wikis, then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly have no idea if -RU articles are being written using GRU-P. Or what the Russian wiki's opinion of the GoI is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whazzaniuswhat? I don't think I have that. :_(

Anyways, I'm glad you like it. I'm new to the site, but I hope I can contribute! : ) Also, do the guys at the -RU Site have anything to say/add about the GRU-P? I don't really know much about other languages. : O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like your headcanon and would like to subscribe to your newsletter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, headcannon time.
Incoming!

GRU-P used 1118 on themselves. At first, they checked they could use it but lacked the finesse to influence people discreetly, subtly enough to avoid raising suspicion in outside countries. However, as the USSR started to visibly degrade, those in charge of 1118 started packing and decided to make sets for the officials in command of the GRU-P section they served under, who would stop them because of the moral complications (and possible technical difficulties; we don't really know what the ritual implies) related to 1118-2, as well as the superiors of those beyond them, effectively making them forego their entire branch of GRU-P. However, some of these deserters were interested in political meddling, while others wanted to start their own private business by manipulating the first big corporate powers that would be interested in Russia.

By 1991, the original GRU-P was pretty much over, and a single survivor, not even an real GRU-P agent, half wishing to shoot himself and be done with it and half wishing to get rid of 1118 and be "free" from the responsibility over the item that turned his partners against each other, decided to come to the Foundation. He was scared of the thing, you see. I wonder if he didn't want to go to the GOC because he never heard of them in the first place…

By the way, GOC and GRU-P should have some story too, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, you got it. It's the ritual that requires things the Ethics Committee apparently doesn't condone more than once. As for GRU-P and what was perhaps the forerunner of OBSKURA, the question remains as to whether they made more sets or perhaps they too were daunted by the ritual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not talking about the Foundation, more like the GRU-P. Although I got that about the target itself after I wrote the comment. I feel silly now. : (

Anyways, for what I read, it's still perfectly possible to create new sets. Obtaining pieces from deceased people shouldn't be a problem for the Foundation; the real problem would come with the method to properly fuse the bone into the typebars, and yet we have the details of the ritual involved. The ethics committee is the only real impediment to repeating the procedure, right? Is there anything else I'm not getting? :_(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't follow. The only one completely believing what they say is the target itself. It doesn't have any effect on listeners.

And there's a reason we're not making other sets. Read carefully. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… then, imagine you make a set for yourself. You have someone write the right words while you are at a small conference with top brass officers, even a simple manifesto with your thoughts in the matter. Nobody suspects you. Suddenly, everyone agrees with you.

This item is perfect for social and political engineering.

Also, radio reproduction? How about recordings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, speech induced by SCP-1118 feels entirely appropriate to targets, even after the fact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By the way, writing this stuff doesn't mean the affected person won't go back on their words eventually, right?

Upvoted. I can see the GRU-P manipulating people within the URSS, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe I'm only seeing this comment now. Sorry about this late reply, wishun.

The thing is, once you have an artifact like this, it's real hard to time its use. You need to make sure whoever you puppet is in a position to dictate policy. In addition, it needs to sound authentic, so that people listening won't go: "THE FUCK IS THIS SHIT?" That, coupled with the fact that Himmler might have been more interested in puppeting his fellow Germans than the enemy, and the Germans' taste for bureaucracy, more than explains it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just stuck on why this wasn't used/abused more, particularly when Himmler had it.
Being able to put words in people's mouths, especially if they then try to live up to those words….you could conquer the world with a mad lib.

"Good <time of day> my fellow <victim nation>-ers, this is your <executive title> speaking. I am pleased to announce that I am immediately departing for <your nation>, where I will sign a historic treaty that will secure the future of <victim nation> for generations to come! Our neighbors and staunch allies from <your nation> have agreed to take over all military defense and internal policing duties. I am therefore ordering the immediate disarmament and disbanding of our now redundant former military forces. Thank you for your honorable service! Thus freeing up much needed tax dollars to solve the pressing <minor local concern> problem plaguing our youth today. This truly marks the dawning of a golden age for <victim nation>. Thank you. Good night and <local deity> bless."

Or, much simpler, just a couple well-timed strings of "Yo Mama" jokes can provoke a war between any nations that may ally against you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Judging from the intensity of the reaction, I'd say the subject I wrote about is something he's very passionate about. That's fine, I'm not going to dispute an opinion that is clearly at least partially emotionally motivated. As long as it's not about me, but about the article, I'm good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you're on the wrong site if you dislike "Hidden history"

Also, that isn't the article at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get that impression at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty much an attempt to "retcon" real life political decisions the author either does not understand or approve of into supernaturally induced behavior with the subtlety and background knowledge of a high schooler.

Consequently, and because this goes too far into hidden history, something I immensely dislike, I find it revolting and downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. Seeing as you're the father of GRU Division "P", I really appreciate the upvote.

One thing though:

I especially liked the attempt to use it after Truman's death.

It wasn't used after his death. I tried that at first, but that posed too many problems. Instead, it was used after his presidency ended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent work, and an amusing explanation for the Munich Treason. You dealt with the concept better than I had anticipated it'd be possible.
I especially liked the attempt to use it after Truman's death.

Maksim says this works.

Overall an enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I seem to have a thing for teeth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's under the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I played around with various keyboard configurations. Problem is, why would you need numbers in speech? And if you're not going to have all 46 keys on the keyboard, would that leave as many keys in each script (Cyrillic, Latin etc.)? To avoid that, I changed it so the keyboard needs to be in the target's native language. And since the target applies a degree of filtering to what he's fed, things like umlauts aren't necessary.

It's a bit like reading 'th1s' and still reading 'this'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Footnote 1 seems to have been orphaned; you might want to look at that. (Either that or I missed it. If so, please tell me where to look…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is, since I love The Jennifer Morgue and the Atrocity Archives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoiler alert
- Were you spoiled?

That's actually what my fingers hit when I mimed being dragged off the keyboard. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The nazis not using it is again is actually pretty simple to explain. Hitler wanted war in 1938, before he got the Munich Agreement(masterminded mostly by alot of quick-thinking and masterful diplomacy by Goering). He felt he had been 'cheated', and 'swindled' out of his war. Come 39, with the conflict over the corridor, he wasn't going to have some last-minute deal brokered to swipe his war from him again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Teeeeeeeeeeth. D:

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay, the toothwriter is back! Glad to see you've shaped this into something pretty memorable. One question, and I'm doubtless showing my ignorance of German typewriters here because I'm sure this is something everyone knows:

Sets of SCP-1118-2 contain all 26 letters of the Latin alphabet

If it was manufactured in Germany, wouldn't it have more letters? Umlauts, ß, etc.? At the very least, there should be more key settings, even if the repurposing was meant for English only.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had the same question as the crab above, and I think there's too little info. I see your rationale for not explaining, but I had the question of how a target is selected in my brain throughout the article-it's all I was looking for. Everything else is laid out in excruciating detail except how the target is selected. Just feels like an omission that doesn't fit the tone of the article. This is very clinical, very direct, very well organized-which makes the lack of this information all the more glaring/distracting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nother' thing. This reminds me of the Atrocity Archives. That's an extremely good thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very well done. Took me a few reads to grasp the implications of this. +1

Also:

[20:25:58] sieojgbkc

made me lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, no problem. A request for help on a draft is not an obligation. I would have loved to have gotten your input too, but I'd reached the point where every edit I made to the draft was beginning to feel like I was overwhipping the cream. So to speak. Thanks for the praise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, and thanks for your invaluable feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoiler alert
- Are you a spoiled one?

The teeth can be anyone's, but the procedure needed to attune a set of prepared molars to their intended target was adapted by the Ahnenerbe from an ancient ritual recovered in the hunt for the roots of the Aryan master race. I only hint at it in the article (and try to imply something by the fact that creating the -D set needed an Ethics Committee sanction) to emphasize the fact that it is *not* a nice thing to do.

I could probably flesh it out in its entirety, but I prefer to leave its shadow hanging over the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I don't understand - how does the typewriter pick its target? The Foundation must know, because they used it on the class d. I assumed it was whoever's teeth was in the typewriter, but I think someone would have noticed that suspiciously German dentist removing Chamberlain's teeth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done - I don't think the Truman transcript was in last time I saw this and it's a really great addition. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry I never got back to you about your draft. I didn't have the facilities all weekend to do a good line-by-line for you like I wanted. :( Most of my problems were with awkward phrasing that have clearly already been resolved. I like this version way more than the last one. Last time it felt like a cheap joke on Pres. Bush Jr. Now it feels like a sinister political manipulation plot, which is much better.

+1. Good work, buddy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Russian wiki translates GRU Division "P" as "ГРУ, Отдел "Психотроника"". Apart from being an already established translation, it's also more accurate: дивизион is almost never used in Russian, with Division being better translated as дивизия or отдел, and while both Психотроника and Психотроники can be translated to English as Psychotronics, the former is a branch of science, while the latter are people with psychic powers and might not actually be a real word.

Seriously, will you believe I completely forgot the Russian wiki would probably have translated that for me? D'OH! Will fix immediately.

Yet that time is never stated in the article.

It's in Addendum 1118-A-1.

Also the article as a whole is very good. It's rare to see history used for horror in a skip, but this one does it really well.

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also a good question: why did the SS never use it again? And did the Russians ever use it? And if not, why not?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has been stenciled over in white paint with "ГРУ дивизион - Психотроники - #12".

The Russian wiki translates GRU Division "P" as "ГРУ, Отдел "Психотроника"". Apart from being an already established translation, it's also more accurate: дивизион is almost never used in Russian, with Division being better translated as дивизия or отдел, and while both Психотроника and Психотроники can be translated to English as Psychotronics, the former is a branch of science, while the latter are people with psychic powers and might not actually be a real word.

Chemical analysis of the ink used on SCP-1118-2 sets and comparison to reference samples provides an approximate time of manufacturing.

Yet that time is never stated in the article.

Efforts to retrieve and contain SCP-1118 are sabotaged by Ahnenerbe and Abwehr operatives, both within and outside of the Foundation.

I love the implications of this.

Also the article as a whole is very good. It's rare to see history used for horror in a skip, but this one does it really well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

The SS made a device capable of, somehow, controlling the speech of a 'target'? Jesus Fucking Christ. Thank the Allfather that that bumbling fool Himmler was running the show, and that Heydrich was killed before he could make his move on power..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that. As I've stated before, yours is amongst the upvotes I particularly value.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: image removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it more now. I particularly love that you went to the effort to craft the letter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MUCH better than the previous version. The historical implications are definitely creepier, and you utilized the GOI very well. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to VAELynx, Meserach, DrSevere and SRegan for helping me rework what once tanked into something that - hopefully - passes muster.
